text.title: Architecture Review Meeting Facilitation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Architecture Review Meeting Facilitation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One meeting facilitation playbook per session; bundling two meetings into one cycle hides per-meeting blockers.
rule.rationale: Per-session bound keeps the agenda and decision log clean and auditable.

rule.statement: Pre-read MUST land ≥24h before the meeting; meetings without pre-read are cancelled, not held.
rule.rationale: No-pre-read meetings degrade into status reads; cancellation is the cheap way to enforce the discipline.

rule.statement: Each agenda item is time-boxed and ordered by decision-needed-by date.
rule.rationale: Time-boxing prevents the loudest voice from monopolizing; date ordering keeps urgent decisions ahead of nice-to-haves.

rule.statement: Decisions, dissent, and follow-ups are captured in a versioned decision log; ad-hoc notes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a structured log, decisions get re-litigated and follow-ups lapse silently.

rule.statement: Each meeting opens with an audit of the previous meeting's follow-ups; closed items are archived, open items reassigned.
rule.rationale: Without audit, follow-ups become aspirational; the audit step is what makes the decision log real.
